title: NBA Daily Digest
subtitle: Your latest NBA news for April 7, 2026

items.headline: Wembanyama Suffers Rib Injury, Regular Season Status in Doubt
items.summary: San Antonio Spurs phenom Victor Wembanyama sustained a left rib contusion during Monday's 115-102 victory over the Philadelphia 76ers, leaving his availability for the remainder of the regular season uncertain. Wembanyama, who had 17 points in 16 minutes, needs to play 20+ minutes in one more game to be eligible for All-NBA and MVP awards.

items.headline: Luka Dončić Out for Season with Hamstring Injury
items.summary: The Dallas Mavericks will be without star guard Luka Dončić for the rest of the regular season due to a Grade 2 left hamstring strain. His status for the playoffs remains uncertain, a significant blow to the Mavs.

items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander Named Western Conference Player of the Week
items.summary: Oklahoma City Thunder's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander has been named the NBA Western Conference Player of the Week, recognizing his outstanding performances.

items.headline: Knicks Edge Hawks in Close Contest
items.summary: Jalen Brunson's 30 points and 13 assists led the Knicks past the Hawks, who saw a massive 36-point effort from Nickeil Alexander-Walker.

items.headline: Magic Dominate Pistons Behind Banchero's Big Night
items.summary: Paolo Banchero dropped 31 points and Desmond Bane added 25 as the Magic cruised past the Pistons. Jalen Suggs chipped in with a near triple-double: 12 points, 12 assists, 6 rebounds, and 3 steals.

items.headline: Cavs Outlast Grizzlies in 3-Point Barrage
items.summary: Evan Mobley scored 24 points and Dennis Schroder tallied 22 points and 11 assists for Cleveland. Memphis tied an NBA single-game record with 29 three-pointers in a losing effort.

items.headline: Spurs Defeat 76ers, Wembanyama Injured
items.summary: The Spurs secured a win against the 76ers, but not without concern as Victor Wembanyama left the game with a rib injury. Joel Embiid led Philly with 34 points and 12 rebounds.

items.headline: Jokic's Triple-Double Leads Nuggets Past Blazers in OT Thriller
items.summary: Nikola Jokic recorded his 33rd triple-double with 35 points, 13 rebounds, and 13 assists as the Nuggets rallied from 16 down to beat the Blazers in overtime. Deni Avdija had 26 points for Portland, and Toumani Camara added 30 points, hitting eight threes as the Blazers set a franchise record with 25 total threes.

items.headline: Jokic Logs 33rd Triple-Double
items.statContext: Led Nuggets to an OT win vs. POR.

items.headline: Deni Avdija Shines for Blazers
items.statContext: Strong scoring effort against the Nuggets.

items.headline: Banchero Dominates with 31 Points
items.statContext: Key to Magic's victory over the Pistons.

items.headline: Alexander-Walker Erupts for 36
items.statContext: Season-high in a tough loss to the Knicks.

items.headline: Embiid's Strong Double-Double
items.statContext: Led the 76ers in scoring and rebounding vs. SAS.

items.headline: Playoff Picture Intensifies as Regular Season Nears End
items.summary: With the NBA regular season in its final week, the playoff and play-in tournament races are heating up across both conferences. The Play-In Tournament is scheduled for April 14-17, with the NBA Playoffs commencing on April 18.

items.headline: Eastern Conference Playoff Race Update
items.summary: The Detroit Pistons (57-21) lead the East, followed by the Boston Celtics (53-25) and New York Knicks (50-28). The Cleveland Cavaliers (49-29), Atlanta Hawks (45-33), and Philadelphia 76ers (43-35) are battling for top spots, while the Toronto Raptors (43-35), Charlotte Hornets (43-36), Orlando Magic (42-36), and Miami Heat (41-37) jockey for play-in positioning.

items.headline: Western Conference Playoff Race Update
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder (62-16) hold the top seed in the West, with the San Antonio Spurs (59-19) close behind. The Los Angeles Lakers (50-28) and Denver Nuggets (50-28) are tied, followed by the Houston Rockets (49-29) and Minnesota Timberwolves (46-32). The Phoenix Suns (43-35), Los Angeles Clippers (40-38), Portland Trail Blazers (40-38), and Golden State Warriors (36-42) round out the playoff and play-in contenders.
